audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra peace/well-being offering text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for sacrifice of peace/well-being, herd/flock/goat options, fat portions, blood/life, and fat/blood prohibition.

verses.restored: If his offering is sacrifice of peace/well-being, if from herd he brings near, whether male or female, complete one he shall bring near before YHWH.
He shall lay his hand on head of his offering and slaughter it at entrance of tent of meeting; sons of Aaron, the priests, shall throw blood/life against altar all around.
He shall bring near from sacrifice of peace/well-being a fire-offering to YHWH: fat covering inward parts and all fat upon inward parts,
two kidneys and fat on them that is by loins, and lobe upon liver; with kidneys he shall remove it.
Sons of Aaron shall turn it into smoke on altar upon burnt offering that is on wood on fire; fire-offering, soothing scent to YHWH.
If his offering for sacrifice of peace/well-being to YHWH is from flock, male or female, complete one he shall bring near.
If he brings near a lamb as his offering, he shall bring it near before YHWH.
He shall lay his hand on head of his offering and slaughter it before tent of meeting; sons of Aaron shall throw its blood/life against altar all around.
He shall bring near from sacrifice of peace/well-being a fire-offering to YHWH: its fat, whole fat tail removed close to backbone, fat covering inward parts, all fat on inward parts,
two kidneys and fat on them by loins, and lobe upon liver; with kidneys he shall remove it.
The priest shall turn it into smoke on altar; food of fire-offering to YHWH.
If his offering is goat, he shall bring it near before YHWH.
He shall lay his hand on its head and slaughter it before tent of meeting; sons of Aaron shall throw its blood/life against altar all around.
He shall bring near from it his offering, fire-offering to YHWH: fat covering inward parts and all fat on inward parts,
two kidneys and fat on them by loins, and lobe upon liver; with kidneys he shall remove it.
The priest shall turn them into smoke on altar; food of fire-offering for soothing scent. All fat is YHWH's.
Age-long statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings: all fat and all blood/life you shall not eat.

translation_notes: Shelamim is peace/well-being/wholeness offering, likely shared meal logic in broader context.
Male or female animals may be brought for this offering, unlike some other categories.
Fat portions belong to YHWH in this ritual grammar; blood/life is not eaten.
Blood/life prohibition should be read as reverence for life, not disgust toward bodies.
safety_note: Do not turn fat/blood prohibitions into body-shame. In this ritual system, fat and blood/life are reserved to YHWH as symbols of abundance and life.
